subject: Mathematics
title: Fermat's Last Theorem
description: Learn Fermat's Last Theorem in one minute.

hook.text: A scribbled margin note tormented the world's best mathematicians for 350 years.

buildup.text: Fermat claimed no three positive integers satisfy a^n + b^n = c^n for n greater than 2.

discovery.text: He wrote that he had a marvelous proof, but the margin was too small to hold it.

twist.text: In 1995, Andrew Wiles proved it after seven years of secret, solitary work.

climax.text: His proof ran 129 pages and used math that didn't exist in Fermat's lifetime.

punchline.text: The margin note that haunted math for three centuries.

quiz.question: Who finally proved Fermat's Last Theorem in 1995?
